mania_5: 6) Agromania: Intense urge to stay in open spaces.
mania_6: 7) Aichmomania: Obsession with sharp or pointed objects.
mania_10: 11) Amaxomania: Obsession with riding in vehicles.
mania_15: 16) Eremiomania: Excessive love of solitude and seclusion.
mania_17: 18) Biliokleptomania: Compulsive urge to steal books.
mania_18: 19) Bibliomania: Obsession with books and/or reading.
mania_20: 21) Cacodemomania: Pathological belief that one is possessed by an evil spirit.
mania_21: 22) Callomania: Obsession with one's own beauty.
mania_22: 23) Cartacoethes: Uncontrollable urge to consult maps anywhere, anytime.
mania_23: 24) Catapedamania: Obsession with jumping from heights.
mania_24: 25) Cheimatomania: Abnormal fondness for cold or cold objects.
mania_26: 27) Clinomania: Excessive desire to stay in bed.
mania_28: 29) Coloromania: Obsession with a particular color.
mania_30: 31) Countermania: Compulsion to experience terrifying scenes.
mania_32: 33) Demonomania: Pathological belief that one is possessed by a demon.
mania_34: 35) Dikemania: Obsession with seeing justice carried out.
mania_41: 42) Empleomania: Endless pathological craving for work.
mania_42: 43) Enosimania: Pathological belief that one is sinful.
mania_46: 47) Gamomania: Obsession with making elaborate marriage proposals.
mania_48: 49) Goetomania: Obsession with witches and witchcraft.
mania_49: 50) Graphomania: Obsession with writing everything down.
mania_51: 52) Habromania: Near-pathological cheerful delusions despite reality.
mania_56: 57) Iconomania: Obsession with icons and portraits.
mania_57: 58) Idolomania: Obsession with an idol, even to the point of devotion.
mania_61: 62) Ligyromania: Uncontrollable urge to make loud or harsh noises.
mania_62: 63) Linonomania: Obsession with string or cord.
mania_63: 64) Lotterymania: Extreme compulsion to buy lottery tickets.
mania_65: 66) Megalithomania: Near-pathological urge to invent strange ideas when standing among stone circles or megaliths.
mania_66: 67) Melomania: Obsession with music or a particular melody.
mania_67: 68) Metromania: Uncontrollable urge to write poetry constantly.
mania_68: 69) Misomania: Obsession with hating a person, thing, or group.
mania_69: 70) Monomania: Near-pathological fixation on a single idea.
mania_71: 72) Nosomania: Delusion of suffering from an imagined disease.
mania_72: 73) Notomania: Compulsion to record everything (e.g. photography).
mania_73: 74) Onomamania: Obsession with names (of people, places, or things).
mania_74: 75) Onomatomania: Uncontrollable urge to repeat a particular word.
mania_76: 77) Opsomania: Pathological craving for a particular food.
mania_77: 78) Paramania: Near-pathological pleasure derived from complaining.
mania_82: 83) ASPD: Pathological urge to violate social morals.
mania_86: 87) Question-Asking Mania: Compulsion to ask questions.
mania_89: 90) Siderodromomania: Fascination with travel by train or rail.
mania_90: 91) Sophomania: Delusion of possessing incredible wisdom.
mania_92: 93) Thanatomania: Belief that one is cursed by death magic.
mania_93: 94) Theomania: Belief that one is a deity.
mania_94: 95) Titillomania: Compulsive urge to scratch oneself.
mania_95: 96) Tomomania: Abnormal fondness for performing surgery.
mania_96: 97) Trichotillomania: Compulsion to pull out one's own hair.
